title: astragalus rubescens
intro: Astragalus rubescens, also known as the red milk vetch or red milkvetch, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the pea family. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries due to its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, which help the body adapt to stress and promote immune function. The medicinal properties of Astragalus rubescens are attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, Astragalus rubescens is often used to boost the immune system, reduce fatigue, and improve overall health, and is typically available in capsule, tea, or tincture form.
